commit.message:
Lower Go baseline to 1.25

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Keep deploy docs anchored to the canonical guides

The README had grown into a second copy of the install and target-host
operator guides, which made the docs harder to keep aligned and blurred
the boundary between orchestration surfaces and the mutable ranctl path.
This change deletes repeated prose from the README, fixes one broken
examples link, and aligns the same boundary language across the docs
hub, the Symphony/Codex/skills architecture note, and the install /
real-server guides.

Constraint: Keep operator commands and proof boundaries unchanged
Rejected: Rewrite the guides into a new docs structure | unnecessary scope expansion for a deslop lane
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep detailed target-host dashboard exposure guidance canonical in 18 and prefer README pointers over duplicate walkthrough text
Tested: npm run docs:build; mise exec -- mix contract_ci; manual cross-link and proof-boundary review across README.md, docs/index.md, and architecture docs 06/12/16/17/18
Not-tested: Remote operator walkthrough execution on a live host
Related: YON-115
Related: YON-112

Co-authored-by: mud-the-developer <mud-the-developer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Deslop ran_action_gateway and ran_config request/policy glue

Collapse the duplicated backend parsing in `RanActionGateway.Request`
and move policy description formatting into `RanConfig.ChangePolicy`
so the runner and config layer share one request/policy boundary.
The cleanup stays inside the selected file cluster and adds focused
regression tests for backend normalization and omitted-target policy
handling before reusing the formatter from the runner.

Constraint: Standard backends must still normalize to atoms while replacement backends stay as request strings
Rejected: Introduce a new shared glue module | adds another boundary for a small cleanup pass
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep request normalization and policy description changes paired with the focused request and policy tests
Tested: mix test apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/request_test.exs apps/ran_config/test/ran_config/change_policy_test.exs apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:220 apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:1112 apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:1134
Tested: mix compile --warnings-as-errors
Tested: mix contract_ci
Not-tested: mix runtime_ci

Co-authored-by: mud-the-developer <mud-the-developer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Filter non-repo Docker containers out of Mission Control

The runtime board was still trusting broad `ran-*` name and compose
project prefixes after the first filtering pass. That let unrelated
stacks from other repositories survive whenever their container names
looked similar to the local OAI or agent lanes.

This change makes the compose working directory the primary ownership
signal by resolving it against the current repository's git common-dir.
Known `ran-oai` and `ran-agent-local` prefixes remain only as a narrow
fallback when Docker does not expose the workdir label. The snapshot
fixture now proves both sides of the contract: repo-local OAI and xApp
lanes stay visible, while foreign `ran-grafana` and `ran-agent-local`
compose workloads are dropped.

Constraint: Runtime board input is limited to `docker ps -a` metadata
Rejected: Keep broad `ran-*` matching | still leaks foreign compose
 stacks from other repos
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Treat compose working_dir as the primary ownership signal;
 only broaden prefix fallback after verifying a repo-owned lane lacks
 that label in production
Tested: `mix test apps/ran_observability/test/ran_observability/
 dashboard_snapshot_test.exs`
Tested: `mix run -e '...YON111ReproRunner...'`
Tested: `bin/ran-dashboard`; `curl http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/health`;
 `curl http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/dashboard`
Not-tested: Playwright PNG capture; local Chrome install required sudo

Co-authored-by: mud-the-developer <mud-the-developer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Use rAF for scroll-sync unlock with timeout fallback and add DOM tests (#1951)

### Motivation

- Replace a simple `setTimeout` unlock in the scroll sync hook with a more robust scheduler that prefers `requestAnimationFrame` to reduce latency and improve performance.
- Ensure pending animation frame and timeout schedulers are cleaned up on unmount to avoid dangling callbacks.
- Add DOM-focused unit tests to validate rAF behavior, timeout fallback, and cleanup semantics.

### Description

- Replaced inline `setTimeout` unlock logic in `useScrollSync` with a `scheduleSyncUnlock` helper that uses `requestAnimationFrame` when available and falls back to `setTimeout` with `SCROLL_SYNC_DEBOUNCE` otherwise.
- Added `rafIdRef` and `timeoutIdRef` refs to track scheduled handles and thread-safely cancel them when rescheduling.
- Added a `useEffect` cleanup that cancels any pending animation frames or timeouts and resets `isSyncingRef`.
- Updated `handleEditorScroll` and `handlePreviewScroll` to call `scheduleSyncUnlock` and adjusted their dependencies accordingly.
- Added new unit test file `tests/unit/useScrollSync.dom.test.ts` which mocks `requestAnimationFrame` and timers to exercise rAF scheduling, timeout fallback, and unmount cleanup behaviors.

### Testing

- Ran the new DOM unit tests under `vitest` using `@testing-library/react`'s `renderHook`, and they verify rAF scheduling, timeout fallback with `SCROLL_SYNC_DEBOUNCE`, and cleanup on unmount; all tests passed.
- Tests specifically include `schedules unlock with requestAnimationFrame when available`, `falls back to timeout when requestAnimationFrame is unavailable`, and `cleans up pending schedulers on unmount`, and were executed with appropriate mocks for `requestAnimationFrame` and timers.
- No other automated test failures were observed when running the updated test suite.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(tests): handle prebuild-generated gradle.properties in SDK 55 test (#989)

* fix(tests): handle prebuild-generated gradle.properties in SDK 55 test

expo prebuild adds newArchEnabled to android/gradle.properties, but
this file is gitignored. Skip the check when the file doesn't exist
and filter out prebuild-injected lines when it does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-storefront): cast ctaLink to satisfy Expo Router typed routes

The HeroSlide ctaLink type expects Expo Router's route union, but
the slides come from dynamic config data as plain strings. Cast
to the expected type since the links are validated at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(jumia): add max_age=0 to force fresh OAuth session on retry (#990)

* fix(tests): handle prebuild-generated gradle.properties in SDK 55 test

expo prebuild adds newArchEnabled to android/gradle.properties, but
this file is gitignored. Skip the check when the file doesn't exist
and filter out prebuild-injected lines when it does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-storefront): cast ctaLink to satisfy Expo Router typed routes

The HeroSlide ctaLink type expects Expo Router's route union, but
the slides come from dynamic config data as plain strings. Cast
to the expected type since the links are validated at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add max_age=0 to force fresh OAuth session on retry

Jumia's /login endpoint detects prior sessions and redirects to
/connect/auth/callback with a stale session id, returning "no route
matched". Adding max_age=0 (OpenID Connect) forces Jumia to ignore
cached sessions and always present a fresh login page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test(cockpit): fix evidence shortcut CI race

Summary:
- wait for evidence rows instead of the section shell in the new cockpit shortcut test
- record the GitHub frontend follow-up in the Batch K implementation note

Rationale:
- the PR frontend check exposed an async race where the section mounted before the workflow and approval evidence rows were populated
- this keeps the test aligned with the real loading boundary instead of local timing luck

Tests:
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Handle bad C6 radical splits gracefully (#419)

Treat inconsistent C6 radical block data as failure, instead of
raising errors: it simply can happen that the initial
determination that we are in C6 case turns out to be wrong, and we
have to abort.

Perhaps there is a deeper issue here with the math in that case,
but for now this seems plausible and fixes a concrete issue.

Fixes #295

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(mobile-admin): use ephemeral browser session for Jumia OAuth (#988)

Jumia's OAuth server retains session cookies that cause "no route matched"
errors on retry when the first attempt fails. Using an ephemeral browser
session ensures each Connect attempt starts fresh with no stale cookies.

commit.message:
fix(mobile-admin): use system default notification sound (#986)

* fix(mobile-admin): use system default notification sound

sound: 'default' tells expo-notifications to look for a custom sound
file named "default" in res/raw/, which doesn't exist. Use null to
play the system default notification sound instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-admin): remove sound: null to use system default sounds

Removing the sound property entirely lets Android use the system
default sound based on channel importance (HIGH channels get sound,
LOW channels stay silent). sound: null explicitly silences channels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Mark PostgreSQL package as side-effectful

The package applies Temporal polyfills at module load time, so its
package metadata must not advertise the module as side-effect free.

Set sideEffects to true so bundlers preserve the initialization when
consumers import the package.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/botkit/pull/19#discussion_r3008937579

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Ignore undefined sql-only option fields

Treat url and maxConnections as conflicting with sql only when
they have non-null values, so option spreads that preserve
undefined fields do not fail at runtime.

Add a regression assertion that verifies undefined mutually
exclusive fields remain accepted.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/botkit/pull/19#discussion_r3008857879

commit.message:
Serialize follower writes with cleanup

Acquire follower-level advisory locks in addFollower() before writing
followers and follow_requests so cleanup and inserts cannot race on
the same actor key.

Add a regression test that reproduces concurrent reassignment plus
new follow insertion and verifies valid follower state is retained.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/botkit/pull/19#discussion_r3008663314

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): migrate raw timingSafeEqual to HMAC-based constantTimeEqual (#982)

* fix(web): migrate raw timingSafeEqual to HMAC-based constantTimeEqual

Replaces raw `timingSafeEqual` with our shared `constantTimeEqual()` utility
across 11 API routes + 1 auth helper. The HMAC approach prevents length-leak
timing attacks by producing fixed-length digests regardless of input.

Also restores the `constant-time-equal.ts` utility that was missing from main,
fixes the agentic auth (was using plain `===`), and adds colocated tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): address CodeRabbit review on constant-time-equal migration

- Add Unicode and null-byte edge-case tests for constantTimeEqual
- Fix cleanup-orders auth check order to validate CRON_SECRET before
  constructing the expected token string

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): use getCronSecret() in cleanup-orders for consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Summary:

commit.message:
refactor(tests): remove dead_code allowances (#10)

## Why
- Reduce `dead_code` suppression noise in the test tree.
- Keep each integration test target compiling only the helpers it
actually uses.

## What
- Split the shared `tests/support/mod.rs` helper into smaller support
modules grouped by use case.
- Update each integration test file to import only the support it needs.
- Replace the test-only `MeasureOptions::with_guess` helper with direct
`MeasureOptions` struct construction.

## Testing
- `cargo check --tests`
- `cargo test anafanafo::tests::anafanafo_matches_reference_cases`
- `cargo test badge_maker_matches_reference_cases`
- `cargo test raw_css_variable_input_falls_back_like_badge_maker`
- `cargo test invalid_id_suffix_matches_badge_maker_error`

## Risks
- This removes the public `MeasureOptions::with_guess` helper. External
callers should construct `MeasureOptions { font, guess }` directly
instead.

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(native): harden release gates and Pi auth

Summary:
- add a first-class native release matrix and wire combined macOS,
  Windows, and Android baseline commands into the plugin workflow
- default provider-connected desktop and Windows parity checks to the
  supported OpenRouter GPT-5.4 Mini path with clearer transient retry
  handling and baseline summaries
- align Pi auth storage, vault path resolution, Studio inventory, and
  desktop automation bridge behavior around the bundled auth backend

Rationale:
- keep release verification anchored to the native harness instead of
  ad hoc manual spot checks
- remove stale direct-OpenAI assumptions from the Windows provider
  lane so release prep uses the provider path that actually works here
- reduce auth and filesystem drift between plugin runtime code,
  Studio inspection, and native automation helpers

Tests:
- npm run check:plugin
- npm run check:release:native
- git diff --check
- gitleaks dir . --no-banner (hits were limited to ignored local files
  and existing build/cache artifacts, not the staged diff)

commit.message:
fix(workflow): keep merging issues routable and dashboards live

Summary:
- add `Merging` to active tracker states so post-review tickets keep
  polling until their PR merge is observed
- update merge handling to move already-merged PRs directly to `Done`
  instead of treating them like stale branch resets
- strengthen the project overview rules so new projects maintain a live
  PR review priority dashboard at the top of the Linear description

Rationale:
- template workflows should not strand merged work in `Merging` just
  because the orchestrator stopped routing that state
- the template should encode the live dashboard behavior explicitly so
  downstream projects do not drift into one-off overview prose

Tests:
- not run (workflow prompt update only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(workflow): keep merging issues routable and dashboards live

Summary:
- add `Merging` to active tracker states so post-review tickets keep
  polling until their PR merge is observed
- update merge handling to move already-merged PRs directly to `Done`
  instead of treating them like stale branch resets
- tighten project overview instructions so the Linear description stays
  a live PR review priority dashboard instead of drifting into a stale
  narrative summary

Rationale:
- StackPerf had several merged issues stranded in `Merging` because the
  workflow stopped polling them after the human state transition
- the live dashboard only helps reviewers if agents keep regenerating it
  in place as queue conditions change

Tests:
- not run (workflow prompt update only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(coverage): cover color API edge cases (#8)

## Why
- Increase coverage on the public color API without changing production
behavior.
- Lock down named color aliases and low-level literal color handling
with integration-style tests.

## What
- Add public API tests that round-trip every `NamedColor` through
parsing and display.
- Verify that named colors and literal colors render the same badge
output as their typed equivalents.
- Cover the fallback behavior for an explicitly constructed invalid
`Color::Hex` value.

- `just test`
- `src/badge_color.rs` reaches 100% line coverage in the generated
`lcov` report.

commit.message:
ci(coverage): upload reports to Codecov (#7)

## Why
- Make Rust test coverage visible in CI and publish it to Codecov.
- Enforce a 30 second limit per `nextest` test case to avoid hung
reference-driven tests.
- Closes #6.

## What
- Install `llvm-tools-preview` and `cargo-llvm-cov` in the existing
`check` job.
- Generate `lcov` coverage during the `nextest` run and upload
`target/llvm-cov/lcov.info` to Codecov via OIDC.
- Keep doc tests in the same CI job without adding a separate coverage
job.
- Tighten the shared `nextest` timeout to 30 seconds per case.

## Testing
- `cargo nextest list --config-file .config/nextest.toml`
- Full CI coverage run not executed locally because this machine does
not have `bun`; the workflow installs `bun` before running the
reference-backed tests.

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent stale resurfacing, weak invalidation, and meta-process chatter (#37)

* fix: prevent stale resurfacing, weak invalidation, and meta-process chatter in heartbeat

Addresses the core bugs described in #33:

1. Resolved fence: decay processor now prevents StateResolved memories from
   transitioning back to active/stale. Resolved memories can only move forward
   (to archived/deleted), closing the zombie resurfacing loop caused by
   access-burst importance boosts.

2. Meta-process filter: PendingWork signals now skip internal agent bookkeeping
   content (monitoring, syncing, cleanup, etc.) so it never reaches users.

3. State-aware dedup with suppression footprint: shouldSuppressTopicRepeat now
   checks recently suppressed decisions (not just "act" decisions) with a 2x
   dedup window, catching state-transition zombies where a memory's state
   changes and the same fingerprint re-enters the pipeline.

4. New GetRecentDecisions store method returns all heartbeat decisions (not just
   "act") within a time window, powering the suppression footprint layer.

Closes #33

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(storage): cover get recent decisions

* test: cover suppression footprint dedup

* fix: tighten meta-process filtering and decay reasons

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
